description: TSMC manufactures all of NVIDIA's advanced GPUs and AI accelerators on its cutting-edge process nodes

financials.note: NVDA paid ~$15B to TSMC in FY2025 (~45% of $33B COGS). NVDA overtook Apple as TSMC's #1 customer in 2025 at 19% of TSMC revenue. Source: TSMC revenue disclosures, NVDA 10-K.

description: Direct competitors in GPU, AI accelerator, and data center markets
Competing in AI accelerators and data center GPUs; Intel is trying to enter the discrete GPU market
Broadcom provides networking chips and custom ASICs that complement NVIDIA's GPU platforms
Marvell supplies data infrastructure silicon used alongside NVIDIA GPUs in cloud data centers
Micron provides HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) used in NVIDIA's data center GPUs

financials.note: MU is #2 HBM supplier to NVDA behind SK hynix. MU FY2024 revenue ~$25B.

description: Supermicro builds server systems featuring NVIDIA GPUs for AI data centers
Dell sells enterprise servers and workstations powered by NVIDIA GPUs

financials.note: Dell enterprise server GPU spend. Dell FY2024 revenue ~$88B. NVDA GPU spend is material but not dominant.

description: Competing in AI processors for mobile/edge — Qualcomm NPU vs NVIDIA Jetson
TSMC uses ASML EUV machines to manufacture NVIDIA chips — indirect critical dependency
SK hynix supplies HBM3E memory used in NVIDIA H200 and B200 AI GPUs

financials.note: SK hynix is dominant HBM3E supplier (>70% share) for NVDA's latest GPUs. SK hynix FY2024 revenue ~$45B.

description: Cadence provides EDA software tools for GPU and AI chip design
Synopsys provides chip design and verification software for NVIDIA GPU development
Broadcom supplies wireless connectivity chips and RF components for iPhones

financials.note: Broadcom disclosed Apple as ~15-20% of revenue (~$7-8B annually). Wireless chips + RF components for iPhones. AVGO FY2024 revenue ~$51B.

description: Qualcomm supplies 5G modem chips for iPhones — Apple developing in-house modem
Apple is TSMC's largest customer; TSMC manufactures all Apple A-series and M-series chips

financials.note: Apple was TSMC's #1 customer in 2024 (24% rev), fell to #2 in 2025 (17%) as NVDA overtook on AI chip demand. Source: TSMC annual filings.

description: Compete in OS, cloud, AI assistants, and productivity software
Compete in mobile OS (iOS vs Android), AI, and services
Google pays Apple billions annually to be default search engine on Safari
Compete in smartphones, tablets, and consumer electronics globally
Samsung supplies OLED displays and memory chips for iPhones and Apple devices
Foxconn (Hon Hai) is Apple's largest manufacturing partner, assembling iPhones

financials.note: Apple is ~55% of Foxconn (Hon Hai) revenue — classic asymmetric dependency. Foxconn handles iPhone assembly and component procurement for Apple.

description: AWS and Azure are the two largest cloud providers
AWS competes with Google Cloud in AI services and digital advertising
Two largest US retailers — e-commerce vs physical + e-commerce
AWS is a top buyer of NVIDIA GPUs for cloud AI instances
Azure vs GCP, Copilot vs Gemini, Bing vs Google Search
Compete in enterprise software, databases, and cloud infrastructure
Oracle databases run on Azure; multi-cloud interoperability partnership
Dynamics 365 vs Salesforce in CRM and enterprise applications
Compete in design, marketing, and document software
Azure AI infrastructure runs on NVIDIA GPUs; Microsoft is a top-3 NVIDIA customer
Two dominant digital advertising players
Compete in social media; Meta has replicated Snapchat features
Meta is a top-3 NVIDIA GPU buyer for AI research and recommendation systems
Google Cloud and DeepMind use NVIDIA GPUs; Google also develops TPU chips
Compete in EVs; Tesla leads while Ford expands its electric lineup
Compete in EVs and autonomous driving technology
Compete in electric truck and SUV market
Palo Alto Networks secures Tesla vehicle and factory networks
Tesla uses NVIDIA GPUs to train Autopilot and Full Self-Driving AI models
Tesla buys lithium from Albemarle for EV batteries
Two largest US banks — retail, investment banking, wealth management
Compete in investment banking, trading, asset management
Compete in retail banking, mortgages, consumer lending
Two dominant global payment networks
Visa network vs PayPal peer-to-peer digital payments
Two largest US integrated oil and gas companies
Global competitors in oil exploration, production, and refining
Compete in vaccines, oncology, and immunology
Compete in pharmaceuticals and consumer health
Two dominant GLP-1 weight loss and diabetes drug makers
Two largest US health insurers
Compete heavily in Medicare Advantage
Two dominant US home improvement retailers
General merchandise retail — Target positions as more upscale
Walmart vs Costco membership warehouse model
Costco vs Walmart's Sam's Club and retail stores
Compete for consumer dining dollars globally
Fast-casual and premium quick-service restaurant competition
Major aerospace and defense contractors
Compete in defense contracting, fighter jets, space systems
Top US defense contractors — missiles, radar, aviation
Two leaders in streaming entertainment
Media, theme parks, streaming — Comcast owns NBCUniversal
Two largest US wireless carriers
Wireless — T-Mobile gaining share from AT&T and Verizon
Two leading US ride-hailing platforms
Uber Eats vs DoorDash — largest US food delivery platforms
Salesforce disrupted Oracle's enterprise software dominance
Enterprise marketing and digital experience platform overlap
ServiceNow and Salesforce compete in enterprise workflow/automation
Leading cybersecurity — endpoint, cloud security, threat detection
Network security and firewall market competition
Zero-trust security and secure access service edge (SASE)
CrowdStrike vs SentinelOne — AI-powered endpoint security
Two largest semiconductor equipment manufacturers
Process control and yield management for semiconductor manufacturing
Applied Materials supplies deposition, etch, and inspection equipment to TSMC fabs

financials.note: TSMC is a top AMAT customer. AMAT FY2024 revenue ~$27B. Semiconductor equipment market is diversified across foundries.

description: Lam Research provides etch and deposition equipment critical to TSMC advanced manufacturing
Both supply critical equipment — Lam: etch/deposition, KLA: inspection
ASML's EUV lithography machines are essential for TSMC's most advanced chip manufacturing

financials.note: TSMC is ASML's largest customer (~30% of ASML revenue). Each EUV machine costs ~$200M. TSMC deploys the most EUV systems globally.

description: Intel relies on ASML's lithography equipment for advanced chip fabrication
Intel competes with TSMC foundry via Intel Foundry Services
AMD relies on TSMC to manufacture Ryzen, EPYC, and Radeon chips
Shell and ExxonMobil — global integrated energy competitors
SoFi challenges traditional banks with digital-first banking, lending, investing
